input_natural_language: The autopilot shall change states from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot is in control (standby).
explanation: The produced LTL property expresses that the autopilot must transition from the NOMINAL state to the STANDBY state as long as the pilot is in control (the standby condition is true). This means that whenever the system is in MANEUVER (not specified directly in the input) or any other state, it will only switch to STANDBY when the standby condition holds. Previous LTL properties may fail to accurately capture the condition of changing states under the specific context of the pilot's control unless they specifically check the transitions based on this condition.

The property ensures that when the autopilot is in the NOMINAL state and the pilot takes control, the system will eventually switch to the STANDBY state.
